title: Samosa

ingredients.title: For the Dough
items: Ghee or neutral oil – 48 g (3 tbsp + 1 tsp)
Water – 80–88 ml (about ⅓ cup + 1 tsp, adjust for firmness)

ingredients.title: For the Filling
items: Potatoes – 320 g (about 2 medium), boiled, peeled, diced small
Green chili – 2 g (½ tsp finely chopped)
Coriander leaves – 8 g (about 2 tbsp finely chopped)
Ghee or oil – 16 ml (about 1 tbsp + 1 tsp)
Garam masala – 1.6 g (¼ tsp + a pinch)
Salt – to taste (about 3–4 g for filling)

instruction.title: Make the Dough
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine flour, ajwain, and salt.
Rub in ghee/oil until it looks like coarse breadcrumbs.
Add water gradually and knead into a firm dough.
Cover with a damp cloth and rest for 30 minutes.

instruction.title: Prepare the Filling
steps: Heat ghee/oil in a pan.
Add roasted spice mix, stir for 10 seconds until aromatic.
Add green chili and ginger, sauté briefly.
Add peas and cook until just tender.
Add potatoes, turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, amchur, and salt.
Toss gently, mashing lightly so filling binds.
Add coriander leaves, remove from heat, cool completely.

instruction.title: Shape the Samosas
steps: Divide dough into 4–5 balls; roll each into a 6-inch oval.
Cut each oval in half to make two semi-circles.
Form a cone from each half, sealing straight edge with water.
Fill with 1½ tbsp filling, leaving a little space at the top.
Seal the open edge firmly, ensuring no gaps.

instruction.title: Frying
steps: Heat oil to 150–160°C (medium-low).
Fry in batches for 6–8 minutes until pale golden.
Raise heat to 170–175°C and fry 1–2 minutes to crisp.
Drain on paper towels.

chefTips: Dough texture: Too soft = oily samosas; too hard = cracks.
Frying temperature: Low-and-slow ensures blistered, crisp shells.
Spice bloom: Always roast whole spices for depth of flavor.
Storage: Par-fry at 150°C for 4–5 minutes, cool, freeze; refry from frozen at 170°C for service.
